text.title: TypeScript Integration Testing with Supertest
text.summary: Node.js API integration testing using supertest with database setup and teardown helpers.

rule.statement: Use beforeAll/afterAll for database setup and teardown. Use beforeEach to create fresh test data — never share rows between tests via module-level state.
rule.rationale: Module-level test data creates ordering dependencies; tests that pass in isolation fail in suite due to state leakage.

rule.statement: Assert HTTP status AND body shape. Never assert only the status code — a 200 with an empty body or wrong schema is a bug.
rule.rationale: Status-only assertions let schema regressions through. Body shape assertions catch contract breaks at the seam.

example.note: No body assertion means a 201 with wrong schema passes the test.
